understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get one or more pushable blocks onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level required moving blocks strategically to reach designated goal positions before running out of steps.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement works by selecting a piece (P on the grid) and moving it in cardinal directions. The grid uses 3 pixels per cell. Pushable blocks (B) can be moved if adjacent; walls (#) block movement and create barriers. Multiple selectable pieces (S) can exist on a level and must be switched between using CLICK at their coordinates. The movement_blocked hints indicate adjacent obstacles but don't guarantee failure. Some puzzle solutions required coordinating multiple pieces or pushing blocks into specific arrangements. The step budget was finite per level, making efficient pathfinding essential.
